windows-gfx1151.blocked_reason: GPU port validated on real gfx1151: cuda_util-ut 48/48 x2 deterministic (core GPU primitives). Full-app/methods-ut e2e blocked by an OUT-OF-SCOPE host-build dual-STL collision: TBB oneapi cache_aligned_allocator pulls MSVC <memory_resource> (absent from bundled libcxxmsvc) -> MSVC std::memory_order collides with libcxxmsvc std::__y1::memory_order under clang-cl. Histogram kernels covered by gfx1100 wave32 (identical source). Windows build needs ~9 local deltas (see notes.md); committing the port-relevant subset (cuda.cmake /FI + Win hygiene defines + /clang:-nostdinc++; root CMakeLists flatbuffers include-order; transform.cu PowVector param-qualifier mangling fix; clang_rt.builtins + advapi32 link) re-validates gfx90a+gfx1100. NEEDS JEFF: commit deltas as delta-port, and/or accept cuda_util-ut + gfx1100 histograms as the gfx1151 gate vs unblocking the e2e (needs a libcxxmsvc <memory_resource>/TBB host fix first).
